page: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with four NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs, providing a total of 320 GB of VRAM. The host system features 48 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.2 GHz, with 24 physical cores and hyper-threading enabled. It includes 680.0 GB of system memory, yielding 14.17 GB per core, alongside 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct performance profile. The server achieves top-tier results in large language model inference, particularly in prompt processing across small, medium, and large models, as well as text generation for medium and large models. Conversely, single-core CPU performance is weak, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tasks. Multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth remain in the average range.

Given the high density of GPU and memory resources, this server type represents a specialized hardware class. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads that can fully leverage parallel GPU acceleration, such as LLM inference, deep learning training, and data science jobs. It is less suitable for single-threaded applications or general-purpose computing where the underutilized GPU hardware would result in resource inefficiency.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for parallel computing workloads. It features four NVIDIA A100 Ampere GPUs with 320 GB of total VRAM, 48 vCPUs on an Intel Xeon x86_64 architecture, 680.0 GB of system memory, and 1608 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark results show top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing and text generation for medium and large models. However, a key tradeoff is its weak single-core CPU performance, which falls into the bottom 25% in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks perform in the average tier. This server is optimized for deep learning, LLM inference, and data science workloads that require dense GPU acceleration, while being less efficient for single-threaded or general-purpose tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-ultragpu-4g features 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 320GB VRAM, 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 680GB RAM, delivering top-tier LLM inference performance alongside weak single-core CPU execution.

tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform combining four NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 680GB RAM for demanding parallel processing and large language model inference.

48 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 680.0 GB of system memory
1608 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds for large language models
Weak single-core CPU performance relative to general-purpose instances
Best suited for LLM inference, deep learning, and parallel data science jobs
